item.claim: lib/test/run.sh's create-issue-contract module boundary lower bound was raised 382 -> 394, and the registry the module runner parses must carry the same operand.

item.claim: coverage-map.json's new "709" entry uses the same object shape ({note, owner}) and owner vocabulary the map's consumer validates, and 'unmodularized' is a legal owner for an issue whose pins DO live in a module.

item.claim: create-issue-contract.sh invokes `python3 $CI_ROOT/scripts/render-audit-prompt.py dispatch-instructions --slug ci709 --draft-path ... --instructions-path ...` — flag names, subcommand name, and required-argument set must match the renderer's argparse.
item.verify_hint: Read the dispatch-instructions subparser: confirm --slug, --draft-path, --instructions-path exist and are the complete required set, and stdout carries the render.

item.claim: The relocated pin asserts the literal `render-audit-prompt.py file --slug` appears in the RENDERED dispatch-instructions output.

item.verify_hint: Render dispatch-instructions from a fixture draft and confirm the exact substring `render-audit-prompt.py file --slug` occurs exactly once in stdout.

item.verify_hint: Grep each pinned literal across skills/create-issue/SKILL.md + references/*.md; each must appear EXACTLY once and not be split across a line wrap (#375).

item.verify_hint: Grep both literals; confirm exactly one occurrence each across $CI_BUNDLE and that the pin target is the bundle, not the render.

item.verify_hint: Read prompt-mass-census.py to learn the metric, then recompute for step-3-6-audit.md and audit-prompt-template.md and compare to 59756 / 14849.

item.verify_hint: Sum python3 len(text.split()) over SKILL.md + the 9 references; compare against 27146 (+-2%) and against the doc's per-file rows summing to the bold total.

item.claim: docs/create-issue-budget.md's default-path figure 30,948 equals the live default-path operand and is under the unmoved 31,262 ceiling AND inside the <=5% ratchet-legality band.
item.verify_against: The default-path measurement + ceiling-legality assertion in create-issue-contract.sh

item.verify_hint: word-split each of the six named references and compare to the table rows; confirm the rows sum to 27,146.

item.claim: run.sh's new ias_instructions() declares `local PATH="${4:-$PATH}"` and assumes python3 and git resolve under the restricted PATH the #546 fixtures pass.
item.verify_against: The $RESTRICTED PATH construction in the #546 block
item.verify_hint: Find where RESTRICTED is built and confirm it contains dirs providing both python3 and git.

item.claim: ias_instructions redirects the generator's stdout into $root/instr-$slug.md and then hashes that file; on a generator failure the redirect leaves a zero-byte file and the function returns the empty-blob hash rather than failing loudly.
item.verify_against: The renderer's fail-closed contract in scripts/render-audit-prompt.py
item.verify_hint: Construct the failure: run the helper's command shape against a title-less draft and observe whether the fixture silently degrades to unestablished instead of failing the suite.

item.claim: run.sh fixtures pass --instructions-file/--instructions-draft-path to record-dispatch and --instructions-object-id/--extra-dispatch-content no to record-return; flag names and value vocabulary must match argparse.
item.verify_hint: Check the four flag spellings and the --extra-dispatch-content choices set in scripts/issue-audit-state.py.

item.claim: run.sh's updated expected outputs pin exact token strings for record-return, eligibility, triggers, and the summary line.
item.verify_hint: Compare each expected string against the emitting f-string / field order — especially that steering/steering_reason render immediately before attestation=.

item.claim: The embed-arm fixture now expects `eligible=yes ground=override` where it previously expected ground=event-ordering; asserts the override ground outranks it and that event-ordering is still covered somewhere.
item.verify_hint: Confirm override outranks event-ordering, that the --kind/--surface values are legal, and check whether any remaining fixture still covers ground=event-ordering.

item.claim: test_python_scripts.py's _round() gained a MUTABLE default argument steering={'state':...,'reason':...} — one dict shared by every fixture round.
item.verify_against: All call sites of _round and any code mutating a round's steering dict
item.verify_hint: Grep for ['steering'] assignments and for _validate/normalizers that mutate the passed dict in place; construct a case where two fixtures share the object.

item.claim: The fixture round shape gained attempts[0]['instructions']=None and a round-level 'steering' key — must match the exact schema _validate enforces for SCHEMA_VERSION 3.
item.verify_hint: Confirm 'instructions' is validated on the attempt and 'steering' on the round, and that None is accepted for both.

item.claim: The #709 malformed-state matrix's 'positive control' is assert_eq(True, True) after a bare _validate(...) call — it would still pass if the preceding line were deleted.
item.verify_hint: Confirm _validate raises on failure so the bare call is the real control; judge whether the True/True assert is coverage-inflating or a deliberate reached-here marker used elsewhere.

item.claim: The row named 'a forged steering pair never grounds an approve-mode eligibility read' actually calls _validate and asserts StateError — it tests validation, not approve-mode eligibility.
item.verify_hint: Confirm no eligibility call appears in that assertion; check whether an equivalent forged-pair state can reach evaluate_eligibility at all (is _validate on every load path?).

item.claim: The #709 test class depends on symbols and reason tokens that must exist: _Run603, hash_bytes, --instructions-template, record-degraded --reason instructions-generation-failed, and eight steering reason tokens.
item.verify_hint: Grep each reason token in scripts/issue-audit-state.py and confirm instructions-generation-failed is in _DEGRADED_REASONS' argparse choices.

item.claim: test_render_audit_prompt.py's DispatchInstructions depends on instructions_bytes/_default_template_path/draft_title/RenderError and issue-audit-state's split_body — signatures and argument order must match.
item.verify_hint: Compare positional argument order of instructions_bytes against the D9 call; confirm draft_title raises RenderError and split_body takes/returns bytes.

item.claim: Test D5 asserts the dispatch-instructions render ignores --extension-file; requires the flag to be accepted (not rejected), else the equality could pass vacuously.
item.verify_hint: Run the two commands D5 issues; confirm withext.returncode == 0 and both stdouts are non-empty — the test asserts neither.

item.claim: The pinned skill sentence asserts a universal: filing is never blocked on ANY arm.
item.source_excerpt: **Filing is never blocked on any arm.**
item.verify_hint: Build the case: a file-arm round with steering not-established, NO recorded override, and a request to emit-body / query-eligibility --mode approve. Observe whether emit-body refuses with empty stdout and whether the skill has an arm reaching an override there.

item.claim: EVERY absence fails closed with its own distinct reason token, never established-clean by omission.
item.source_excerpt: Every absence fails closed with its own reason token
item.verify_hint: Enumerate every path into steering_state and construct an input for each absence combination NOT covered by the four tested rows (instructions recorded but file deleted before return; both flags absent simultaneously; a migrated schema-2 round with no steering key). Observe whether a distinct reason is emitted or one silently wins.

item.claim: Regenerating from the recorded closed inputs reproduces the dispatched bytes exactly, so a legitimate clean audit never false-alarms.
item.verify_hint: Run dispatch-instructions twice from DIFFERENT cwds, with relative vs absolute --draft-path, and after touching the template's mtime; check whether any rendered value derives from os.getcwd(), the environment, or a path not recorded as a closed input.

item.claim: schema 2 -> 3 in the changeset and DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW must match SCHEMA_VERSION, and a schema-2 state on disk must have a defined migration/rejection path.
item.verify_hint: Confirm SCHEMA_VERSION is 3 and check what load_state does with an on-disk schema_version of 2 (migrate, refuse, or silently accept — a silent accept would let a schema-2 round reach the new gate with no steering key).

item.claim: The cutover record claims 'None' for grants and probes, lib/capability-profiles.json untouched, and no install.sh coupling.
item.verify_hint: Confirm lib/capability-profiles.json, lib/review-profile.tokens, install.sh, and .github/workflows/* are absent from the diff.

item.claim: The #614 routing-table<->references reconciliation must still pass and audit-prompt-template.md's exemption from it must be real.
item.verify_hint: Read the #614 block: confirm its exemption set contains audit-prompt-template.md and that every reference the diff touched still has a routing row.

item.claim: The new #709 pins use `# structural-pin-ok: <reason>` markers; the #666 mutation-routing gate's format must accept these exact lines, including on a multi-line-continued call.
item.verify_hint: Read the marker parser: confirm a trailing marker on the same LOGICAL line satisfies the format-strict check for the multi-line-continued call too.

item.claim: The new #709 pin literals must not also appear in a comment of their pinned target (the #370 pin-in-comment lint).
item.verify_hint: Check whether the lint's scope includes docs/ and .changeset/; confirm no pinned literal appears in a comment of its target.

item.claim: The #603 regression rows' expected trigger output changed to reason=steering-unestablished — every pre-existing _Run603 fixture now trips the new arm, possibly masking the original findings-based trigger those rows asserted.
item.verify_hint: Determine whether reason= reports a single winning token; if steering-unestablished preempts the findings reason, check whether any remaining row still asserts the pre-#709 findings reason distinctly.

item.claim: The shadow_round_rows expectation flipped to t2=hold reason=steering-unestablished with the comment 'the only trigger is the embed arm's by-construction steering state' — the attribution must be correct for that fixture's arm.
item.verify_against: The SR fixture's recorded arm and evaluate_triggers
item.verify_hint: Find the fixture setup and confirm the round uses the embed/inline arm; if it is a file arm, the trigger is really inputs-unrecorded and the comment misattributes the cause.

item.claim: An in-process fixture expects steering_reason=no-instructions-file while the CLI row for a dispatch that recorded no instructions expects inputs-unrecorded — if nothing distinguishes the two conditions, one expectation is wrong.
item.verify_hint: Compare the CLI row _noinp709 (dispatch with_instructions=False) expecting inputs-unrecorded against the in-process fixture expecting no-instructions-file. Read the branch and determine what distinguishes them (arm token? recorded instructions=None vs missing key?).

item.claim: regenerate_instructions_digest calls mod.instructions_bytes with 5 positional args whose order/arity must match render_instructions' signature.
item.verify_against: The real signature in the generator
item.verify_hint: Compare the call against `def render_instructions(...)` and `def instructions_bytes(...)` — confirm positional order (template_path, slug, draft_path, instructions_path, draft_text) matches exactly.

item.claim: _load_generator asserts exactly the entry points instructions_bytes and default_template_path exist as module-level public names.
item.verify_hint: Grep for `def instructions_bytes` and `def default_template_path`; confirm neither is nested or renamed and that _default_template_path does not shadow the public one.

item.claim: hash_bytes(rendered_instruction_bytes) must equal the object ID an auditor gets from `git hash-object --no-filters <file>` on the file the orchestrator wrote from the CLI's stdout.
item.verify_against: hash_bytes and main()'s dispatch-instructions stdout write
item.verify_hint: Confirm the bytes written to disk are exactly instructions_bytes(...) including the single trailing newline and that stdout encoding/newline translation cannot add a CR.

item.claim: Regeneration reproduces the dispatched bytes only if {TEMPLATE_PATH} resolves to the same string at dispatch and at regeneration (dispatch records None; regeneration substitutes default_template_path()).
item.verify_against: How the CLI resolves template_path vs default_template_path()
item.verify_hint: Confirm str(template_path) in main() is byte-identical to str(default_template_path()); a resolved-vs-unresolved or symlinked spelling divergence would false-alarm every clean audit.

item.claim: The `di` template block is selected by token 'di' — the block-set parse must yield 'di' as a token member, and no other mode may accidentally match it.
item.verify_hint: Confirm the block-set parse splits on whitespace into a container where token matching (not substring matching) applies, and that render_dispatch mode='file' does not select the di block.

item.claim: The di block's renderer-invocation fence contains the literal `--slug <slug>` which the slots dict substitutes; the emitted command must carry the real slug, and other <slug> occurrences in the di block must also be intended for substitution.
item.verify_against: _substitute's slot map and the di block text
item.verify_hint: Check the slots dict in render_instructions against the di block; confirm <slug> is replaced everywhere it appears in that block, including the out-of-bounds derivation path.

item.claim: draft_title() is a coupled mirror of issue-audit-state.py's split_body title rule — both must agree on which line is the title.
item.verify_hint: Compare leading-blank-skip / bare `#` / `# ` / `##` handling line-for-line; note whether split_body strips the line before testing the prefix (draft_title uses raw.strip()).

item.claim: cmd_record_return passes args.instructions_object_id and args.extra_dispatch_content to steering_state; argparse dest names must match and both must default to None when omitted.
item.verify_hint: Confirm the parser defines both flags (choices yes/no for the affirmation) and that no default= makes an omitted --extra-dispatch-content read as 'no'.

item.claim: cmd_record_dispatch compares Path(args.draft_file).resolve() against Path(args.instructions_draft_path).resolve(); args.draft_file must be guaranteed non-None before this call or Path(None) raises TypeError, escaping the OSError handler.
item.verify_hint: Trace cmd_record_dispatch from entry to the resolve() line: confirm a prior guard fails closed when --draft-file is absent on the file arm.

item.claim: The new summary fields steering and steering_reason render immediately before the contractually-trailing attestation= token.
item.verify_hint: Check cmd_query_summary's print and the suite pins asserting attestation=<token>$ at end-of-line; confirm _SUMMARY_FIELDS order matches the printed order.

item.claim: Every token the new output lines emit (instructions_digest, steering, steering_reason) is a member of _PROTOCOL_TOKENS.
item.verify_hint: Find what enforces _PROTOCOL_TOKENS and confirm the new key= tokens all pass it.

item.claim: The new degraded reason instructions-generation-failed is accepted by record-degraded's argparse --reason choices, not merely present in _DEGRADED_REASONS.
item.verify_hint: Confirm its choices are derived from _DEGRADED_REASONS rather than a hardcoded list.

item.verify_against: All in-repo references to the audit-state schema version
item.verify_hint: git grep SCHEMA_VERSION / schema_version across tests, modules, and docs; confirm none still pins 2.

item.verify_against: All marker-enumeration sites and the contract pins
item.verify_hint: git grep -n the literal across skills/, docs/, lib/test/ and confirm every site uses the same spelling.

item.verify_against: The assertion count the flight recorder computes
item.verify_hint: Find the counting rule and derive the count over lib/test/modules/create-issue-contract.sh to confirm it is at least 394.

item.claim: The path rendered into the instruction file (--instructions-path) and the path record-dispatch hashes (--instructions-file) must be the same file; a mismatch must not regenerate cleanly.
item.verify_hint: Check whether cmd_record_dispatch validates that --instructions-file equals the {INSTRUCTIONS_PATH} baked into the file's bytes; confirm a mismatch cannot regenerate cleanly.

item.claim: The auditor's return-contract line prefixes in the template are the exact strings step-3-6-audit.md tells the orchestrator to read and forward.
item.verify_against: The forwarding prose in step-3-6-audit.md and any contract pin
item.verify_hint: Compare the prefixes in the di block against the forwarding sentence and any pin in create-issue-contract.sh.

item.claim: evaluate_triggers' new #709 arm is reachable and does not fire spuriously on pre-#709 states.
item.verify_against: The full if/elif chain of evaluate_triggers
item.verify_hint: Confirm the preceding unadjudicated-round arm does not already swallow every REVISE round, and that a pre-#709 state does not now fire a spurious T2 offer on every existing clean run.

item.claim: The generator carries no run-varying token, so a regeneration from the round's recorded inputs reproduces the dispatched bytes exactly.
item.source_excerpt: It carries no timestamp, nonce, or other run-varying token
item.verify_against: Falsification: construct the dispatch and the regeneration and compare bytes
item.verify_hint: Invoke dispatch-instructions twice and once via regenerate_instructions_digest with template_path=None and diff the bytes; probe a relative vs absolute --draft-path, a symlinked checkout, and a draft whose title contains a {DRAFT_PATH}-shaped literal.

item.claim: No free-text parameter reaches any rendered block — every slot is shape-checked at the parse boundary or substituted last from file content.
item.source_excerpt: No free-text parameter reaches any rendered block
item.verify_against: Falsification: a draft title crafted to inject content or a slot token
item.verify_hint: Construct the falsifying case: a draft whose title contains {DRAFT_PATH}, a newline-bearing or fence-closing title, and a title containing a render-block-end marker; render and observe whether DRAFT_TITLE-last substitution prevents re-scanning and whether the emitted file's marker positions survive.

item.claim: Every failure mode of _load_generator raises _DigestError so none escapes as a traceback that would abort record-return before it saves the round.
item.source_excerpt: Every failure mode — file absent, unimportable (for ANY reason its module body raises...), or importable-but-missing either entry point — raises ``_DigestError``
item.verify_against: Falsification: drive each failure shape through record-return
item.verify_hint: Point the sibling generator at (a) a missing file, (b) a module body raising SystemExit or KeyboardInterrupt (neither caught by except Exception), (c) a module lacking instructions_bytes; run record-return each time and observe whether the round is still saved and regeneration-failed printed.

item.claim: Filing is never blocked on any arm — only the coverage-backed clean grounding is withheld when steering is unestablished.
item.source_excerpt: **Filing is never blocked on any arm.**
item.verify_against: Falsification: an unestablished-steering run walked through Step 4's gate
item.verify_hint: Construct: a file-arm round with VERDICT: FILE, zero findings, no revision, no --instructions-file; run query-eligibility --mode approve, record-override --kind user-decline --surface step4-approval-after-exhausted-offer, re-query — confirm eligible=yes is reachable (the override ground must not itself be gated by steering) and emit-body then emits non-empty bytes.

item.claim: _clean_identity's docstring asserts the embed/inline arms can never reach the event-ordering ground because established steering is impossible there — intentionally unreachable rather than dead code.
item.source_excerpt: ``evaluate_eligibility`` reaches this ground only when steering was established, which the file-arm-only instruction file makes impossible on the embed/inline arms today
item.verify_against: Falsification: a state whose clean round is embed/inline with steering established
item.verify_hint: Can steering_state ever return established for an attempt whose arm != file? Then check whether the event-ordering ground being unreachable is a behavior REGRESSION for embed/inline runs that previously got eligible=yes on that ground.

item.claim: summary_fields' _require assertions read the steering record against closed vocabularies; the rendered steering_reason falls back to the literal 'none' while summary_fields returns None.
item.verify_against: The _require helper's failure mode and the summary-line consumers
item.verify_hint: Read _require — confirm whether it raises (aborting a read-only query the contract says must exit 0 with one decided token) or emits a breadcrumb; confirm steering_reason=none is the rendered token for a round with no steering record.
